title: Setup documentation: install connector, configure CI, bootstrap instance, create first admin
description: Write the primary onboarding documentation walking a new team through the full setup: standing up an instance, installing the connector in their component repo, configuring CI, and logging in for the first time.
acceptanceCriteria: `docs/setup.md` (or `README.md`'s main body) covers, in order: (1) pulling/running the image via TASK-59's compose file, (2) setting required env vars including bootstrap admin credentials, (3) first login, (4) installing `@systembook/connector` in the design system repo, (5) writing a first `*.preview.tsx`, (6) configuring CI per TASK-45's example, (7) generating an upload token via TASK-44's admin screen
Each step includes the exact command or UI action needed, not just prose description
Doc explicitly calls out that this is a single-instance-per-design-system deployment (no multi-tenant setup steps)
A person unfamiliar with the codebase can follow the doc top-to-bottom and end up with a working instance connected to a real component repo

steps.details: Document pulling the published image, creating `.env` from the documented variable list (TASK-59), and running `docker-compose -f docker-compose.production.yml up -d`.

Document navigating to the instance URL and logging in with `INITIAL_ADMIN_EMAIL`/`INITIAL_ADMIN_PASSWORD`, and recommend immediately creating a named admin/editor user and rotating the bootstrap credentials via TASK-15's reset flow.
Document `pnpm add -D @systembook/connector` (or npm/yarn equivalents) in the design system repo, and the minimal `*.preview.tsx` example referencing TASK-61's schema docs.
Link directly to TASK-45's GitHub Actions example, walking through generating an upload token (TASK-44) and setting the two required secrets.

Read through the full document as if a new user, confirming no step assumes undocumented prior knowledge, and that every referenced command/UI element actually exists per the corresponding implementation task.

technicalNotes: This is the document validated by the PRD's Fase 7 exit criterion: 'uma pessoa de fora do projeto consegue, seguindo só a documentação, subir uma instância e conectar um repo próprio' — treat gaps found during the step-5 review as blocking, not optional polish.
